text.en: Give paracetamol weight-based dose now (10–15 mg/kg).
Tepid sponging with lukewarm water; do NOT use cold water or alcohol.
Refer to PHC/CHC now; rule out cerebral malaria, severe sepsis, dengue shock.

rules.rationale: Core temperature >= 40 °C risks febrile seizures, dehydration, and signals severe infection or heatstroke; same-day facility evaluation required.

text.en: Refer to PHC/CHC for malaria smear/RDT, dengue/typhoid serology, and TB screening.

rules.rationale: Fever >= 7 days warrants laboratory workup for malaria, typhoid, dengue, and TB per IDSP and RNTCP guidelines.

text.en: Isolate patient if measles is suspected; notify ANM/MO for IDSP reporting.
Refer to PHC/CHC for CBC + NS1 antigen and platelet trend.

rules.rationale: Fever with rash + petechiae raises concern for severe dengue / measles; both are IDSP-notifiable and require platelet monitoring.
